theme_note: Rigid light partitions soften under excess load; a surface vibrates between gate and membrane
events:
A machine deciding who may receive light, and who must apply for it.

The body drifts in weightlessness while the ground beneath the stationary burns.

Something rebounds — not recovery, just the reflex of a system flinching back toward its own shape.

Gates are drawn around the garden: who may eat, who may stay, who was never soil to begin with.

The weather has no enemy and no negotiation, only arrival.
context_felt:
A beam bowing under brightness it was never meant to hold this long.

The air thickens into a skin — everything ripe past the moment of release, refusing to fall.

Two equal weights pressing from opposite ends of a plank balanced on nothing.
